meta.note: Rule set for predicting der/die/das by suffix or semantic category. Exceptions listed here mirror the article; the app filters the exception list shown on cards to those present in the local word bank.

der.suffix_er.name: Suffix -er
der.suffix_er.description: Most nouns ending in -er are masculine — agent nouns formed from verbs (der Fahrer, der Lehrer, der Spieler), instrument nouns (der Bohrer, der Drucker, der Toaster), and many primitives (der Sommer, der Hammer, der Hunger). Three groups break the pattern and need to be memorized: (a) family/role words for women always take die (die Mutter, die Tochter, die Schwester); (b) several native nouns with non-agent -er stems are die (die Mauer, die Feier, die Nummer, die Dauer, die Feder, die Leber, die Schulter, die Steuer, die Trauer, die Oper); (c) a sizeable group is neuter — most -tier, -wasser, -zimmer, -fenster, -wetter, -alter, -theater, -lager, -fieber, -opfer, -muster, -wunder, -ufer, -kloster, -semester, -fenster, -fenster, -gewässer compounds plus primitives like das Tier, das Wasser, das Zimmer, das Wetter, das Theater, das Meer, das Klavier, das Fieber, das Foyer, das Atelier, das Quartier, das Revier (note the recurring -ier loan ending). Compound -er nouns (X-führer, X-zimmer, X-wasser, …) inherit the article of the rightmost head, not the suffix.

der.suffix_ismus.name: Suffix -ismus
der.suffix_ismus.description: Nouns ending in -ismus are always masculine. Examples: der Kapitalismus, der Journalismus, der Tourismus.

der.suffix_ant.name: Suffix -ant
der.suffix_ant.description: Most nouns ending in -ant are masculine. Examples: der Demonstrant, der Elefant, der Mutant.

der.suffix_ling.name: Suffix -ling
der.suffix_ling.description: Most nouns ending in -ling are masculine. Examples: der Lehrling, der Schützling, der Frühling.

der.suffix_ner.name: Suffix -ner
der.suffix_ner.description: Most nouns ending in -ner are masculine. Examples: der Rentner, der Schaffner, der Zöllner.

der.suffix_or.name: Suffix -or
der.suffix_or.description: Most nouns ending in -or are masculine. Examples: der Motor, der Traktor, der Direktor.

der.masculine_persons.name: Masculine persons, roles, professions
der.masculine_persons.description: Nouns denoting male persons, family roles, or male professions take der. Examples: der Vater, der Pilot, der Arzt, der Bruder, der König.

der.seasons.name: Seasons
der.seasons.description: The four seasons are masculine: der Frühling, der Sommer, der Herbst, der Winter. Note: das Frühjahr (the alternative word for spring) is neuter.

der.months.name: Months
der.months.description: All months are masculine: der Januar, der Februar, der März, … der Dezember.

der.days.name: Days of the week
der.days.description: All weekdays are masculine: der Montag, der Dienstag, …, der Sonntag.

der.compass.name: Compass directions
der.compass.description: Cardinal and intercardinal directions are masculine: der Norden, der Süden, der Osten, der Westen, der Nordwesten, etc.

der.precipitations.name: Precipitations
der.precipitations.description: Forms of precipitation are masculine: der Regen, der Schnee, der Hagel, der Nebel, der Tau.

der.car_brands.name: Car brands
der.car_brands.description: Car brand names are masculine: der Audi, der BMW, der Mercedes, der VW.

der.trains.name: Trains
der.trains.description: Train (type) names are masculine: der IC, der ICE, der RE.

der.verb_derived_no_suffix.name: Verb-derived nouns (no suffix)
der.verb_derived_no_suffix.description: Nouns formed from a verb stem without an added suffix are usually masculine. Examples: der Gang (gehen), der Fang (fangen), der Wurf (werfen), der Schlaf (schlafen).

der.alcoholic_beverages.name: Alcoholic beverages
der.alcoholic_beverages.description: Most alcoholic drinks are masculine: der Wein, der Cognac, der Whiskey, der Schnaps, der Wodka, der Rum, der Sekt, der Likör. The grain spirit der Korn is masculine and follows the rule (das Korn is a different word — the grain/cereal kernel, unrelated to drinks).

der.rivers_outside_europe.name: Rivers outside Europe
der.rivers_outside_europe.description: Most non-European rivers are masculine: der Amazonas, der Mississippi, der Kongo, der Nil.

der.mountains.name: Mountains
der.mountains.description: Most mountains are masculine: der Mont Blanc, der Kilimandscharo, der Montblanc.

die.suffix_falt.name: Suffix -falt
die.suffix_falt.description: Nouns ending in -falt are feminine. Examples: die Vielfalt, die Einfalt.

die.suffix_heit.name: Suffix -heit
die.suffix_heit.description: All nouns ending in -heit are feminine. Examples: die Freiheit, die Sicherheit, die Krankheit.

die.suffix_keit.name: Suffix -keit
die.suffix_keit.description: All nouns ending in -keit are feminine. Examples: die Möglichkeit, die Schnelligkeit, die Einsamkeit.

die.suffix_schaft.name: Suffix -schaft
die.suffix_schaft.description: All nouns ending in -schaft are feminine. Examples: die Freundschaft, die Mannschaft, die Wirtschaft.

die.suffix_t_verb.name: Suffix -t (from verbs)
die.suffix_t_verb.description: Nouns ending in -t that are derived from a verb are feminine. Examples: die Fahrt (fahren), die Tat (tun), die Sicht (sehen), die Flucht (fliehen).

die.suffix_ung.name: Suffix -ung
die.suffix_ung.description: All nouns ending in -ung are feminine. Examples: die Leitung, die Zeitung, die Wohnung, die Bedeutung.

die.suffix_ade.name: Suffix -ade
die.suffix_ade.description: Nouns ending in -ade are feminine. In practice these are nearly all French/Italian loanwords: die Marmelade, die Limonade, die Hitparade, die Parade, die Blockade.

die.suffix_age.name: Suffix -age
die.suffix_age.description: Nouns ending in -age are feminine. Two unrelated groups give the same outcome: (a) native German verb-derived nouns such as die Lage, die Frage, die Sage, die Klage, die Plage, die Waage and their compounds (die Niederlage, die Anfrage, die Aussage, die Anklage, …); and (b) French/Italian loanwords with the foreign -age suffix such as die Garage, die Passage, die Etage, die Massage. Both groups land on die, but the mechanism is different — don't let the article's 'foreign suffix' label mislead you on native-looking words.

die.suffix_anz.name: Suffix -anz
die.suffix_anz.description: Latin/French loanwords ending in -anz are feminine: die Eleganz, die Dominanz, die Toleranz, die Bilanz. Native German words that happen to end in -anz (der Tanz, der Kranz, der Glanz, der Schwanz) are NOT from this suffix — they're masculine.

die.suffix_enz.name: Suffix -enz
die.suffix_enz.description: Latin-origin -enz loanwords are feminine: die Existenz, die Tendenz, die Konferenz, die Konkurrenz. The archaic native masculine der Lenz ('spring') is the lone exception.

die.suffix_ik.name: Suffix -ik
die.suffix_ik.description: Loanwords ending in -ik (from Greek/Latin) are feminine: die Kritik, die Musik, die Politik, die Technik, die Fabrik. Beware: non-suffix -ik endings differ — agent nouns in -iker are masculine (der Katholik), and Germanic -streik words are masculine (der Streik).

die.suffix_ion.name: Suffix -ion
die.suffix_ion.description: Latin-derived -ion loanwords (from Latin -io, -ionis) are feminine: die Diskussion, die Koalition, die Information, die Nation. Greek-origin -ion neuter nouns do NOT follow this pattern — das Stadion, das Symposion, das Anion, das Ganglion. Animate/agent -ion loans take der (der Spion, der Champion, der Skorpion).

die.suffix_taet.name: Suffix -tät
die.suffix_taet.description: Nouns ending in -tät (from Latin -tas/-tatis, the abstract-quality suffix) are feminine. These are all loanwords — German has no native -tät: die Identität, die Qualität, die Universität, die Aktivität, die Realität.

die.suffix_ur.name: Suffix -ur
die.suffix_ur.description: Latin-derived -ur loanwords are feminine: die Agentur, die Reparatur, die Natur, die Kultur, die Literatur. Beware: native German -ur stems (der Schwur, der Flur) are masculine, many -ur loans from other routes are neuter (das Abitur, das Futur, das Interieur), and the French agent suffix -eur is a separate pattern (→ der Ingenieur, der Friseur) with its own rule.

die.suffix_e.name: Suffix -e
die.suffix_e.description: Most nouns ending in -e are feminine. Examples: die Grenze, die Lampe, die Blume, die Sonne.

die.suffix_ei.name: Suffix -ei
die.suffix_ei.description: Most nouns ending in -ei are feminine. Examples: die Abtei, die Metzgerei, die Polizei, die Bäckerei.

die.suffix_ie.name: Suffix -ie
die.suffix_ie.description: Most nouns ending in -ie are feminine. Examples: die Diplomatie, die Psychologie, die Industrie, die Theorie.

die.suffix_in.name: Suffix -in
die.suffix_in.description: Most nouns ending in -in (especially feminine job/role suffixes) are feminine. Examples: die Ärztin, die Studentin, die Freundin.

die.feminine_persons.name: Feminine persons, roles, professions
die.feminine_persons.description: Nouns denoting female persons, family roles, or female professions take die. Examples: die Mutter, die Schwester, die Ärztin, die Königin.

die.motorcycle_brands.name: Motorcycle brands
die.motorcycle_brands.description: Motorcycle brand names are feminine: die Harley Davidson, die Yamaha, die BMW (as motorcycle), die Kawasaki.

die.planes_ships.name: Planes and ships
die.planes_ships.description: Names of planes and ships are feminine: die Boeing 747, die Titanic, die Queen Mary.

die.cardinal_numbers.name: Cardinal numbers (as nouns)
die.cardinal_numbers.description: Cardinal numbers used as nouns are feminine: die Eins, die Zwei, die Drei, die Hundert, die Million.

die.plants_trees.name: Plants and trees
die.plants_trees.description: Most plants and trees are feminine: die Birke, die Chrysantheme, die Rose, die Tulpe, die Eiche.

das.suffix_ial.name: Suffix -ial
das.suffix_ial.description: Nouns ending in -ial are neuter. Examples: das Material, das Potenzial, das Sozial.

das.suffix_ment.name: Suffix -ment
das.suffix_ment.description: Most nouns ending in -ment are neuter. Examples: das Instrument, das Parlament, das Dokument, das Argument.

das.suffix_nis.name: Suffix -nis
das.suffix_nis.description: Most nouns ending in -nis are neuter. Examples: das Ergebnis, das Tennis, das Zeugnis, das Ereignis.

das.suffix_o.name: Suffix -o
das.suffix_o.description: Most nouns ending in -o are neuter. Examples: das Auto, das Konto, das Büro, das Kino.

das.suffix_tum.name: Suffix -tum
das.suffix_tum.description: Most nouns ending in -tum are neuter. Examples: das Quantum, das Ultimatum, das Eigentum, das Wachstum.

das.suffix_um_latin.name: Suffix -um (Latin origin)
das.suffix_um_latin.description: Nouns of Latin origin ending in -um are neuter. Examples: das Publikum, das Museum, das Stadium, das Zentrum.

das.prefix_ge.name: Prefix Ge- (collective / verbal abstract)
das.prefix_ge.description: Collective and abstract nouns formed with the Germanic Ge- prefix (often Ge- + verb stem, frequently with an -e ending) are neuter. This is the productive Ge- pattern: das Gebäude (build → building), das Gebirge (mountain range), das Gemüse (vegetables), das Geschirr (dishware), das Gepäck (luggage), das Gewölbe (vault), das Gefieder (plumage), das Geräusch (noise), das Gewässer (body of water), das Geschwister (siblings), das Geschenk (gift), das Geheimnis (secret), das Gericht (dish/court), das Gesetz (law), das Gebiet (area), das Gefühl (feeling). Two systematic ways the rule fails: (1) some primitive Ge- nouns are masculine (der Geist, der Genuss, der Geschmack, der Geruch, der Gesang, der Gewinn, der Gedanke, der Gehorsam) or feminine (die Geste, die Gerade, die Gerste, die Geige, die Gemeinde, die Geburt, die Geduld, die Gestalt, die Geschichte). (2) Many words happen to start with 'Ge' without containing the Germanic Ge- prefix at all — Greek/Latin loans like Generation, General, Genie, Genetik, Geometrie, Geographie are NOT Ge- collective nouns and follow their own suffix rules (e.g., Generation → -ion → die). Compound Ge- nouns (Geschäftsführer, Gegenstand, Gesetzgeber, …) inherit the article of their rightmost head and are NOT covered by this rule.

das.diminutives.name: Diminutives (-chen, -lein)
das.diminutives.description: All diminutives formed with -chen or -lein are neuter, even when the base word is masculine or feminine. Examples: das Mädchen, das Kaninchen, das Fräulein, das Büchlein.

das.nominalized_infinitive.name: Nominalized infinitives
das.nominalized_infinitive.description: Any German verb infinitive can be used as a noun (capitalized), and it is always neuter. Examples: das Essen (to eat → food), das Schreiben, das Leben, das Laufen.

das.nominalized_adjective.name: Nominalized adjectives (abstract sense)
das.nominalized_adjective.description: Adjectives turned into abstract nouns (the quality itself) are neuter. Examples: das Gute, das Böse, das Schöne, das Neue.

das.colors.name: Colours
das.colors.description: Colours as nouns are neuter: das Rot, das Gelb, das Blau, das Grün, das Schwarz. The colour das Orange is neuter and follows the rule; die Orange is the unrelated word for the fruit and falls under the -e suffix rule.

das.chemical_elements.name: Chemical elements
das.chemical_elements.description: Nearly all of the ~112 chemical elements are neuter. Examples: das Aluminium, das Kupfer, das Uran, das Helium, das Eisen.

das.metals.name: Metals
das.metals.description: Most metals are neuter: das Blei, das Messing, das Zinn, das Gold, das Silber, das Eisen.

das.fractions.name: Fractions
das.fractions.description: Fractions (except one half) are neuter: das Drittel (⅓), das Viertel (¼), das Zehntel, das Hundertstel.

der.suffix_eur.name: Suffix -eur (French agent suffix)
der.suffix_eur.description: Nouns of French origin ending in -eur are masculine agent nouns. Examples: der Ingenieur, der Regisseur, der Friseur, der Chauffeur, der Amateur. (Not in the Passion4teq article; added for accuracy because -eur would otherwise be mis-matched against the feminine -ur rule.)
